req.title: Reset employee password — variant 5
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four key dimensions: (1) valid token + valid password + valid 2FA → success; (2) expired token → 410 rejection; (3) password policy violation → 422; (4) the 30-minute expiry boundary (token at exactly 30 min). Tests are allocated as: positive=happy-path with 2FA (EP/STC), negative=expired token (NEG), negative=policy violation (DT), boundary=token at expiry edge (BVA). Each test is idempotent by generating fresh tokens via fixtures and tearing down state via API cleanup.

tests.name: test_valid_token_2fa_resets_password_and_invalidates_sessions
preconditions: Employee account exists with known credentials
A valid, unexpired reset token has been issued within the last 5 minutes
Employee has an active session cookie stored
2FA is enrolled and TOTP code is obtainable from <totp_fixture>
actions: Enter a valid TOTP code from <totp_fixture>
expected: API GET /api/session returns 401 for previously stored session cookie
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_valid_token_2fa_resets_password_and_invalidates_sessions(
    page, <valid_reset_token_fixture>, <totp_fixture>, <api_client_fixture>, <session_cookie_fixture>
):
    token = <valid_reset_token_fixture>
    old_session = <session_cookie_fixture>
    new_password = "NewP@ssw0rd!2024"

    page.goto(f"/password/reset?token={token}")
    page.fill("[data-testid='new-password']", new_password)
    page.fill("[data-testid='confirm-password']", new_password)
    page.fill("[data-testid='totp-code']", <totp_fixture>.now())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-reset']")

    expect(page).to_have_url("/login", timeout=5000)

    resp = <api_client_fixture>.get("/api/session", cookies={"session": old_session})
    assert resp.status_code == 401, "Old session must be invalidated"

    login_resp = <api_client_fixture>.post("/api/login", json={"password": new_password})
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Login with new password must succeed"

    old_login = <api_client_fixture>.post("/api/login", json={"password": "OldP@ssword99!"})
    assert old_login.status_code == 401, "Old password must be rejected"

tests.name: test_expired_token_returns_410
preconditions: A reset token was issued more than 30 minutes ago (expired)
The token has not been used
actions: Submit POST /password/reset with the expired token and a valid new password and a valid TOTP code
trace: R-WEB-106: expired links must be rejected

tests.name: test_password_failing_policy_returns_422
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists
actions: Submit POST /password/reset with valid token, valid TOTP, but a weak password ('abc') that violates length and complexity policy
expected: Response body lists password policy violations

tests.name: test_token_at_exactly_30_minutes_is_rejected
preconditions: A reset token was issued exactly 30 minutes ago (at the expiry boundary)
The token has not previously been used
actions: Submit POST /password/reset with the boundary-age token, a policy-compliant new password, and a valid TOTP code
expected: HTTP 410 Gone (token at exactly 30 min is expired, boundary is exclusive)
trace: R-WEB-106: email link expires after 30 minutes
R-WEB-106: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

def test_token_at_exactly_30_minutes_is_rejected(
    <api_client_fixture>, <totp_fixture>, <token_factory_fixture>
):
    # Token created at exactly T-30min (boundary: expiry is exclusive at 30min)
    issued_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
    boundary_token = <token_factory_fixture>.create(issued_at=issued_at)

    payload = {
        "token": boundary_token,
        "new_password": "B0undary!Pass#99",
        "totp_code": <totp_fixture>.now()
    }

    resp = <api_client_fixture>.post("/password/reset", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Token at 30min boundary must be expired (410), got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "TOKEN_EXPIRED", (
        f"Expected TOKEN_EXPIRED error, got: {body}"
    )

tests.name: test_token_at_29min_59sec_is_accepted
preconditions: A reset token was issued 29 minutes and 59 seconds ago (one second before boundary)
actions: Submit POST /password/reset with the near-boundary token, a policy-compliant new password, and a valid TOTP code
expected: HTTP 200 or redirect confirming successful password reset
trace: R-WEB-106: email link expires after 30 minutes — token just inside validity window must succeed

tests.name: test_invalid_totp_code_blocks_reset
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists
Employee provides a syntactically valid but incorrect TOTP code
actions: Submit POST /password/reset with valid token, valid new password, but incorrect TOTP code '000000'
expected: Response body contains error referencing 2FA or OTP failure
trace: R-WEB-106: action requires two-factor authentication confirmation
